title: Cursor
description: A practical cheat sheet for engineers using Cursor, focusing on API parameters, CLI flags, and prompt techniques.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the model's output to be more deterministic.
commands.scenario: When building a code review bot that needs deterministic output.

commands.description: Uses the GPT-4o model with streaming output for faster response.
commands.scenario: When you need quick feedback during live coding sessions.

commands.command: Think step by step. Then verify your answer.
commands.description: Encourages the model to reason through problems methodically.
commands.scenario: When solving complex algorithmic challenges that require logical reasoning.

commands.description: Limits the response length to control cost and focus on concise answers.
commands.scenario: When generating short code snippets or explanations.

commands.description: Enables detailed logging for debugging purposes.

commands.command: Use examples to clarify your request.
commands.description: Improves model understanding by providing context through examples.
commands.scenario: When the model needs to generate code based on specific patterns.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for API requests to prevent hanging processes.
commands.scenario: When integrating with systems that require quick responses.

commands.command: Use comments to guide the model's output.
commands.description: Instructs the model to follow specific coding styles or guidelines.
commands.scenario: When generating code that must adhere to team standards.

commands.description: Retries the API call up to 3 times in case of failure.
commands.scenario: When network instability might cause intermittent API failures.

commands.description: Increases the randomness of the model's output for creative tasks.
commands.scenario: When brainstorming new ideas or generating creative content.

commands.command: Use specific function names to guide code generation.
commands.description: Helps the model generate code that matches existing codebases.
commands.scenario: When extending functionality in a large codebase with specific naming conventions.

commands.description: Simulates the command without making actual changes.
commands.scenario: When testing configuration changes or new integrations.

commands.command: Use bullet points to structure your request.
commands.description: Encourages clear and organized responses from the model.
commands.scenario: When generating documentation or structured data outputs.

commands.description: Sets the logging level to info for general operational logging.
commands.scenario: When monitoring API usage without overwhelming detail.

commands.description: Uses the semantic indexing feature to scan the entire repository for context-aware answers rather than just the current file.
Instruct the model to output only the specific lines of code that need changing to minimize token usage and prevent full-file rewrites.
Create a project-specific .cursorrules file in the root directory to enforce permanent coding standards, architectural patterns, and library preferences.
Explicitly attach file context to the chat to prevent the model from hallucinating APIs or using outdated versions of local modules.

commands.command: Iterative Refinement Loop: 'Refactor the above for [constraint]'
commands.description: A prompt pattern used to pass the previous output back into the context window to apply specific constraints like 'memory efficiency' or 'O(n) complexity'.
